articles.title: Heat stress in children's sports: UAE doctors, parents weigh risks as temperatures climb
articles.description: Pediatricians emphasised that parents should look beyond performance and focus on behavioural cues; some children may hide symptoms and try to push through when playing sports

articles.title: Opinion: How the Amish think about vaccines, health care costs, and much more
articles.description: There may be nearly 2 million Amish Americans by 2075. That has large public health implications.

articles.title: Opinion: I led the U.S. CDC response to the 2014 Ebola epidemic. The new outbreak needs massive, immediate, meticulous action
articles.description: “Ebola is an unforgiving enemy,” writes Tom Frieden, who led the U.S. CDC response to the 2014-2016 Ebola outbreak.

articles.title: Dermatologists' guide to bacne: What causes unsightly and painful spots on your back and how to get rid of them... fast
articles.description: With summer fast approaching and flights taking holidaymakers to sun-soaked destinations, the last thing people want to worry about is the spots on their back. But for millions, it's a real concern.

articles.title: How many of your emotions can YOU name? Psychotherapist explains why knowing what you're feeling is the first step to avoiding mental overwhelm
articles.description: Many people find it hard to name their feelings - but being able to identify your emotions is one of the secrets to not becoming overwhelmed by them.

articles.title: Sun safety tips for the summer
articles.description: With the hot summer weather rolling in, there’s no better time to get outside; however, the CEO and Chief Medical Officer of the Eastern Ontario Health Unit says those who will be spending time outdoors this summer, should take precautions before they soak in the sunrays.

articles.title: Our Tommy, 4, fought cancer so hard, and still had the energy to sign ‘goodbye’ to his brothers before his death
articles.description: THE night before four-year-old Tommy Squibbs passed away, he woke up in his hospice bed, looked at his older brothers, and said goodbye. He was non-verbal, but the message he spelled out using sign language was unmistakable: "Goodbye”. Mum of three, Charlie Squibbs, 41, from Rochdale, says: “Tommy was the most perfect gift but one...

articles.title: Medical misinformation on Tylenol in pregnancy spreads faster than safety studies
articles.description: A new Canadian practice paper says the latest, highest-quality evidence is reassuring for pregnant people who need acetaminophen. The episode is a high-profile example of why so-called null findings — studies that find no link or no difference — matter.

articles.title: High cost of living in Sept-Îles has newcomers rethinking future on Quebec's North Shore
articles.description: Nick Hounga and Yabouline Djiwagui moved to Quebec’s North Shore to help with the staffing shortage in health care. Four years later, they’re considering leaving due to the region's high cost of living.

articles.title: Legionnaires' disease was discovered 50 years ago. Here's what experts say we've learned since then
articles.description: The 1976 legionnaires' disease investigation revealed how modernization, through things like HVAC systems, can unintentionally increase the possibility of exposure to environmental pathogens — and how critical it is to widely share public health updates.

articles.title: Dozens of people sickened with deadly bacteria after drinking contaminated raw milk
articles.description: Nearly 60 Idaho residents have fallen sick from drinking raw milk, which the CDC warns can carry deadly bacteria despite advocates' claims of health benefits.

articles.title: The Look Ahead: June 5, 2026
articles.description: CNBC brings you fast, accurate, and actionable business news and market updates.

articles.title: 4 minutes of exercise a day could help control blood sugar
articles.description: Washington Post: Why short 'exercise snacks' can help with blood sugar levels.

articles.title: Researchers identify hidden self-harm histories using machine learning
articles.description: Important mental health history is often present in medical records but hard to find, especially when it is missing from the diagnosis codes that clinicians, researchers and health systems use to search and count conditions.

articles.title: Researchers challenge the traditional understanding of how histone deacetylase inhibitors function
articles.description: Researchers at Baylor College of Medicine and collaborating institutions are challenging the traditional understanding of how cancer drugs called histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitors function.

articles.title: Integrated care reduces hospital visits for adults with disabilities
articles.description: University of Cincinnati and Ohio State University researchers have found that adults with developmental disabilities who have integrated care were less likely to go to the emergency room or be hospitalized than others who were not.

articles.title: Three decades of progress since the discovery of senescence-associated beta-galactosidase
articles.description: A new review was published in Volume 18 of Aging on May 15, 2026, titled "Blue period – features of senescence 30 years after beta-galactosidase."

articles.title: Ex teacher, 55, drops 2 dress sizes without 'giving up anything' on 80% off app
articles.description: With a long history of weight cycling, Sam Matthews used the Simple app to lose weight and improve her long term health goals

articles.title: New open-access COVID-19 dataset supports reproducible clinical research
articles.description: The Biostatistics Unit at the Germans Trias i Pujol Research Institute (IGTP), in collaboration with researchers from several Catalan healthcare and research institutions, has published the DIVINE study database in Scientific Data, a Nature Portfolio journal.

articles.title: Novel technique enables rapid sequencing of rare hantaviruses
articles.description: Infections by hantaviruses are rare but dangerous, killing 30-40% of infected people. When cases occur, public health officials need rapid, detailed information about the virus to identify the strain and its origin, so they can stop others from being exposed to the disease.

articles.title: Manitoba releases its first recommendations on nurse-to-patient ratio
articles.description: With Manitoba being the first province in Canada to legislate the nurse-to-patient ratio, the committee in charge of implementing it released its first recommendations.

articles.title: Manitoba union wants nurse-patient ratios set as soon as possible
articles.description: The union representing nurses in Manitoba says front-line staff want to see nurse-patient ratios set as soon as possible after legislation passed earlier this week.

articles.title: Scientist discover early warning sign breast cancer could spread
Nevada proposes pausing licensing for new hospice and home-health providers while it probes possible fraud
articles.description: Nevada is proposing a months-long moratorium on new licenses for hospice and home-health providers as well as a pause of new enrollments into the state’s Medicaid program while the authorities probe possible fraud, officials announced Friday.

articles.title: Bluejay Diagnostics genomför privat placering på 8,5 m USD
articles.description: Bluejay Diagnostics genomför privat placering på 8,5 m USD

articles.title: 'Trapped in your own body': Family shares ongoing ALS battle, hope to raise awareness on terminal disease
articles.description: While May was ALS Awareness month and several events were held during the month, a Utah family is continuing to try to raise awareness, speaking on their experiences with the terminal disease.

articles.title: Plant foods contain a compound that may strengthen the gut barrier
articles.description: Results from a mouse study suggest that a natural plant compound, phytic acid, shows potential to support gut barrier health, which could possibly provide a protective effect against 'leaky gut'.

articles.title: Dr Kgoete: Filling the gaps of a failing system
articles.description: South Africa's healthcare system is plagued by delays and inequality, leaving many in limbo. Dr Kgoete's work through the Khayalami Health Foundation highlights the urgent need for reform and the human stories behind the statistics.

articles.title: Jim Cramer on the importance of a diversified portfolio
articles.description: 'Mad Money' host Jim Cramer on why today's market action underscores the importance of diversification,

articles.title: Lightning Round: I like Reddit, says Jim Cramer
articles.description: 'Mad Money' host Jim Cramer weighs in on stocks including: Starwood Property Trust, Ralliant, Borgwarner, Reddit, and Blue Owl.

articles.title: 'Our mum survived cancer misdiagnosis - but dad then took his own life'
articles.description: Mary Crowley was wrongly diagnosed with terminal cancer - and husband David could not cope with their ordeal.

articles.title: Zealand phase 2 data on petrelintide shows durable weight loss
articles.description: Phase 2 data show Zealand Pharma’s petrelintide drove up to 10.7% weight loss at 42 weeks. Read more here.

articles.title: South Africa rolls out twice-yearly HIV prevention drug
articles.description: South African officials on Friday announced that the HIV prevention drug Lenacapavir is now available there, calling it a "turning point."

articles.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo could top 20,000 cases in worst case, CDC says
articles.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda could lead to more than 20,000 cases and over 2,000 deaths in a worst-case scenario, according to a CDC report.

articles.title: What’s Going Around in Metro Detroit: Asthma flare-ups, seasonal allergies, eczema, stomach viruses
articles.description: Here’s our weekly round-up of what illnesses are spreading the most in Metro Detroit communities, according to our local doctors and hospitals.

articles.title: McDonald's to test AI drive-thru system at five locations
articles.description: McDonald's announced this week that it is piloting a new artificial intelligence-powered operating system called ArchIQ, including a voice-based drive-thru assistant, as part of a broader business strategy unveiled at its Worldwide Convention in Las Vegas.

articles.title: Marvell Technology and Flex to join S&P 500 effective June 22
articles.description: CNBC's Pippa Stevens talks Marvell Technology and Flex joining the S&P 500.

articles.title: Parasitic illness sickens dozens in Kingston area
articles.description: Health officials in Kingston, Ont., are reporting an outbreak of an intestinal illness that has sickened dozens of residents recently, but are struggling to discover the source.

articles.title: 'Dangerous Laughter' podcast wants to be the late-night comedy America is missing
articles.description: A.J. Rice, CEO of Publius PR and an author, has launched the "Dangerous Laughter" podcast to fill a role that late-night comedy has missed, "Seen, Heard & Whispered" learned.

articles.title: How Boeing is ramping up 737 Max production with a new assembly line
articles.description: After years of delays, Boeing is increasing production of it's best selling airplane the 737 Max. The company is adding a 4th assembly line at its plant north of Seattle. CNBC's Phil LeBeau got a first hand look and spoke with Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg about how the company plans to produce and deliver more airplanes.

articles.title: Fresh concerns emerge over impact of Labor's tax changes on medical tech
articles.description: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

articles.title: Scientists unveil 'living bandage' that could dramatically speed wound healing
articles.description: Rice University researchers developed a "living bandage" that uses engineered cells to deliver healing proteins directly to wounds in animal trials.

articles.title: Meta weighs big equity raise after blockbuster Google deal
articles.description: CNBC's Julia Boorstin reports on news regarding Meta.

articles.title: Federal Reserve needs to deal with this inflation, says KPMG's Diane Swonk
articles.description: Diane Swonk, KPMG chief economist, joins 'The Exchange' to discuss if the jobs report is weighing on equity markets, the impact of a Fed rate hike and much more.

articles.title: SpaceX likely to purchase Cursor in the near future, says 22V's Dauvin Peterson
articles.description: Dauvin Peterson, 22V Research, joins 'The Exchange' to discuss the important parts of the SpaceX story, the company's valuation and much more.

articles.title: How Mel B Connects Zumba to Mental Health: It ‘Has Nothing to Do With Perfection’ (Exclusive)
articles.description: 'When you’re in an environment like a Zumba class, that’s so welcoming and has nothing to do with perfection, you will feel safe,' Mel exclusively told Hollywood Life after she led a dance class at SXSW London.

articles.title: What is Alzheimer’s disease? The symptoms and causes explained after Jon Snow diagnosis
articles.description: Alzheimer's disease, a physical illness that progressively damages the brain, stands as the most prevalent cause of dementia in the UK

articles.title: Bio-Rad receives MicroVal validation for EZ-CheckTM Listeria testing kits
articles.description: Bio-Rad Laboratories, Inc., a leader in life science research and clinical diagnostics products, today announced that MicroVal, the international certification organization for the validation and approval of alternative methods for the microbiological analysis of food and beverages, has approved EZ-Check Listeria spp. and EZ-Check Listeria monocytogenes Kits for the detection of Listeria spp. and Listeria monocytogenes in food products and environmental samples.

articles.title: What is lenacapavir? Everything you need to know about the HIV prevention injection
articles.description: We look at how lenacapavir, a groundbreaking HIV prevention injection, is set to transform the fight against HIV and why it matters for millions worldwide.

articles.title: Novel artificial intelligence technology improves eye disease diagnosis
articles.description: Non-invasive eye scans allow doctors a zoomed-in, three-dimensional look beneath the eye's surface without causing discomfort or pain to the patient.
